message: Last winter, my kid asked why I kept a battery-powered radio in the hall closet. 'Just in case,' I said — pretty vague, right? Then an ice storm knocked out power for two days. That little radio became our news source, weather updates, and honestly, a morale booster. We huddled around it like people used to do, and suddenly it didn't feel like a crisis; it felt almost cozy. The batteries I'd stashed away 'just because' actually mattered. Sometimes the smallest prep decision is the one that saves your afternoon (or your sanity). Have you got a 'random thing you prepped for' that actually came through when you needed it?
